heading: Distressed, at-risk, and economically strong counties
label: 14526 Distressed, at-risk, and economically strong counties

children.label: A
children.text: designate as “distressed counties” those counties in the Appalachian region that are the most severely and persistently distressed;

children.label: B
children.text: designate as “at-risk counties” those counties in the Appalachian region that are most at risk of becoming economically distressed; and

children.label: i
children.text: “competitive counties”, which shall be those counties in the region that are approaching economic parity with the rest of the United States; and

children.label: ii
children.text: “attainment counties”, which shall be those counties in the region that have attained or exceeded economic parity with the rest of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: conduct an annual review of each designation of a county under paragraph (1) to determine if the county still meets the criteria for the designation; and

children.label: B
children.text: renew the designation for another one-year period only if the county still meets the criteria.

children.heading: .—Distressed Counties
children.label: b .—Distressed Counties
children.text: In program and project development and implementation and in the allocation of appropriations made available to carry out this subtitle, the Commission shall give special consideration to the needs of counties for which a distressed county designation is in effect under this section.

children.heading: .—Competitive counties
children.label: 1 .—Competitive counties
children.text: Except as provided in paragraphs (3) and (4), assistance under this subtitle for a project that is carried out in a county for which a competitive county designation is in effect under this section shall not be more than 30 percent of the project cost.

children.heading: .—Attainment counties
children.label: 2 .—Attainment counties
children.text: Except as provided in paragraphs (3) and (4), amounts may not be provided under this subtitle for a project that is carried out in a county for which an attainment county designation is in effect under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: a project on the Appalachian development highway system authorized by ;section 14501 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: a local development district administrative project assisted under ; orsection 14321(a)(1)(A) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: at least one of the participating counties is designated as a distressed county under this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the project will be of substantial direct benefit to at least one distressed county.

children.label: i
children.text: a significant pocket of distress in the part of the county in which the project is carried out.

children.label: ii
children.text: a significant potential benefit from the project in at least one area of the region outside the designated county.

children.heading: .—Reports to congress
children.label: B .—Reports to congress
children.text: The Commission shall submit to the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives an annual report describing each waiver granted under subparagraph (A) during the period covered by the report.
